scope_note: QA Langlands Dedekind Zeta Factorization cert [410]. Primary sources: Neukirch, J. (1999) 'Algebraic Number Theory' doi:10.1007/978-3-662-03983-0 (Dedekind zeta factorization via Kronecker symbol, Ch.VII §5); Hecke, E. (1920) doi:10.1007/BF01453601 (L-functions of CM type); Neukirch-Schmidt-Wingberg (2008) 'Cohomology of Number Fields' doi:10.1007/978-3-540-37889-1 (Kronecker symbol as Frobenius at unramified primes). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): For F=Q(sqrt5) and unramified prime p (p not 5), the Dedekind zeta function factors as zeta_F(s)=zeta(s)*L(s,chi5) where chi5 is the Kronecker symbol (5/p). Equivalently at each prime p: (C1) chi5(p)=+1 for p≡1,4 mod 5 (split) and chi5(p)=-1 for p≡2,3 mod 5 (inert), as computed by kronecker_5(p)=p%5 arithmetic (integer only); (C2) at split p the local numerator is (1-Y)^2=[1,-2,1] (zeta(s) contributes 1/(1-p^{-s}) and L(s,chi5) contributes 1/(1-p^{-s}) when chi5=+1); (C3) at inert p the local numerator is 1-Y^2=[1,0,-1] (zeta contributes 1/(1-p^{-s}) and L contributes 1/(1+p^{-s}) when chi5=-1); (C4) in both cases the product of norms of all primes above p equals p^2=p^[F:Q]: split gives N(P)*N(Pbar)=p*p=p^2, inert gives N((p))=p^2. This p^2 universally matches the leading coefficient a4(P_p)=p^2 in AI Euler factors certs [404] (split) and [409] (inert), tying ζ_F arithmetic into the GL_4/Q Langlands ladder. Theorem NT: Kronecker symbol computation uses only p%5 (integer mod) — no float observer projections enter QA state. Four checks, 44 primes total (22 split, 22 inert). All 44 PASS. Derived 2026-06-13.
